resources.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
resources.description: Comprehensive book covering distributed systems fundamentals, replication, partitioning, and consistency models.

resources.title: System Design Primer
resources.description: An organized collection of resources to help you learn how to build systems at scale.

resources.title: MIT 6.824: Distributed Systems
resources.description: Graduate-level course on distributed systems covering fault tolerance, replication, and consistency.

resources.title: Scalability for Dummies
resources.description: A beginner-friendly series explaining the core concepts behind building scalable web applications.

resources.title: The Art of Scalability
resources.description: Practical guide to scaling organizations, processes, and technology for growing companies.

resources.title: Harvard CS75 - Scalability Lecture
resources.description: David Malan's lecture on scalability covering vertical and horizontal scaling, caching, and load balancing.

resources.title: Introduction to Modern Load Balancing
resources.description: Overview of modern load balancing and proxying concepts including L4 vs L7 balancing.

resources.title: NGINX Load Balancing Documentation
resources.description: Official NGINX documentation on HTTP load balancing methods and configuration.

resources.title: HAProxy Documentation
resources.description: Comprehensive documentation for HAProxy, a widely used open-source load balancer and proxy server.

resources.title: Caching Strategies and How to Choose the Right One
resources.description: Explains common caching strategies like cache-aside, write-through, and write-behind with trade-offs.

resources.title: Redis Documentation
resources.description: Official documentation for Redis, a popular in-memory data store used for caching and messaging.

resources.title: Design of a Modern Cache
resources.description: Deep dive into the architecture of Caffeine, a high-performance Java caching library.

resources.title: CMU Database Course - Intro to Database Systems
resources.description: Andy Pavlo's comprehensive course on database internals covering storage, indexing, and query processing.

resources.title: Use The Index, Luke
resources.description: A guide to database indexing and SQL performance for developers, covering B-trees and query optimization.

resources.title: Database Internals
resources.description: Book exploring the internals of modern database and storage engine architectures.

resources.title: Apache Kafka Documentation
resources.description: Official documentation for Apache Kafka, a distributed event streaming platform for message queues and real-time data pipelines.

resources.title: RabbitMQ Tutorials
resources.description: Step-by-step tutorials for learning message queue patterns with RabbitMQ including work queues and pub/sub.

resources.title: Understanding Message Queues
resources.description: Video explaining message queue concepts, use cases, and comparisons between popular message brokers.

resources.title: CAP Theorem Explained
resources.description: Clear explanation of the CAP theorem and its implications for distributed system design choices.

resources.title: Raft Consensus Algorithm Visualization
resources.description: Interactive visualization of the Raft consensus algorithm for understanding leader election and log replication.

resources.title: Distributed Systems for Fun and Profit
resources.description: Free online book covering distributed systems fundamentals including time, order, replication, and impossibility results.

resources.title: Designing Instagram - System Design Case Study
resources.description: Walkthrough of designing a photo-sharing service covering storage, feeds, and scaling considerations.

resources.title: How Netflix Serves Millions of Users
resources.description: Video exploring Netflix's architecture including CDN, microservices, and resilience patterns.

resources.title: Grokking the System Design Interview
resources.description: Course with real-world system design examples including URL shortener, Twitter, and Dropbox designs.
